name: plugin-forge
description: Create and manage Claude Code plugins with proper structure, manifests, and marketplace integration. Use when creating plugins for a marketplace, adding plugin components (commands, agents, hooks), bumping plugin versions, or working with plugin.json/marketplace.json manifests.
capabilities: Generate complete plugin directory structure with a single command
Create properly formatted plugin.json and marketplace.json manifests
Bump semantic versions across both manifest files simultaneously
Provide reference documentation for plugin architecture and workflows
Support multiple plugin patterns: framework, utility, and domain plugins
Automate README generation with installation and usage templates
author: softaworks
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 256 pattern matches that are all false positives. The 'external_commands' findings are Markdown code blocks in documentation, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are references to hash algorithms in documentation context. The filesystem operations are legitimate scaffolding file creation. No security risks identified.
valueStatement: Creating Claude Code plugins requires proper directory structure, manifest files, and version management. This skill automates plugin scaffolding with scripts that generate correct plugin.json manifests, README templates, and handle semantic versioning across both plugin and marketplace manifests.
userTitle: Build and Manage Claude Code Plugins
limitations: Requires local marketplace root directory to be initialized first
Does not validate plugin code quality or functionality
Version bumping requires manual git commit and tag creation

useCases.targetUser: Developers building their first Claude Code plugin
useCases.title: Plugin Developer Onboarding
useCases.description: New developers use the scaffold script to create their first Claude Code plugin with proper structure, avoiding common manifest errors and ensuring marketplace compatibility from day one.

useCases.title: Marketplace Maintenance
useCases.description: Maintainers use bump_version.py to ensure version consistency across plugin.json and marketplace.json during releases, preventing version drift and installation issues.

useCases.targetUser: Engineering teams with multiple internal plugins
useCases.title: Team Plugin Standardization
useCases.description: Engineering teams use the skill to enforce consistent plugin structure across multiple internal plugins, ensuring all team members follow the same conventions and patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Create a New Plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Claude Code plugin called 'my-plugin' for the marketplace at /path/to/marketplace. The author is 'John Doe' with email 'john@example.com'. The plugin is described as 'A productivity plugin for task management' with keywords 'productivity, tasks, management' and category 'productivity'.

promptTemplates.title: Bump Plugin Version
promptTemplates.prompt: Bump the version of plugin 'my-plugin' in marketplace /path/to/marketplace. Use a minor version bump for the new feature I'm adding.

promptTemplates.title: Add Component to Plugin
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add a new command to my existing plugin. Show me the correct directory structure and file format for adding a '/docs:generate' command that creates documentation from code comments.

promptTemplates.title: Plugin Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my plugin structure at /path/to/plugin and compare it against the reference patterns. Identify any missing components or structural issues that might prevent marketplace registration.

bestPractices: Always use bump_version.py to update versions to ensure consistency between plugin.json and marketplace.json
Follow kebab-case naming convention for plugin names to ensure compatibility
Test plugins locally with /plugin install before submitting to shared marketplaces
antiPatterns: Do not manually edit version numbers in only one manifest file - this causes version drift
Avoid creating plugins directly in the marketplace without using the scaffold script - manual creation often misses required fields
Do not skip the local testing step - plugins with malformed manifests can cause installation failures

faq.question: What is the difference between plugin.json and marketplace.json?
faq.answer: plugin.json lives inside your plugin directory and describes the plugin itself. marketplace.json is the registry that lists all available plugins in a marketplace. Both are required for a plugin to be installable.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to publish to the official Claude Code marketplace?
faq.answer: This skill helps you prepare plugins for any marketplace. For the official marketplace, you will need to follow the specific submission process documented by Anthropic after using this skill to create your plugin structure.

faq.question: What plugin patterns are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports three patterns: Framework plugins for framework-specific guidance, Utility plugins for tools and commands, and Domain plugins for domain-specific knowledge. Each has a recommended directory structure documented in the references.

faq.question: How do I test my plugin before publishing?
faq.answer: Use the /plugin marketplace add command to add your local marketplace root, then install your plugin with /plugin install plugin-name@marketplace-name. Make changes, uninstall, and reinstall to test updates.

faq.question: What happens if I forget to bump the version?
faq.answer: Claude Code may cache the old version. Always bump the version when making changes, even for documentation updates, to ensure users get the latest version.

faq.question: Can I convert an existing skill into a plugin?
faq.answer: Yes. Create a new plugin structure, then move your SKILL.md into the skills/ directory. Update the plugin.json with appropriate metadata and register it in marketplace.json.
